overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from GTA V showing the character Franklin walking across a large concrete bridge in an urban environment. The character proceeds from a bridge overlook past a delivery truck and construction workers toward a commercial intersection with a gas station.

segments.scene_description: The character walks along a concrete bridge walkway with a stone balustrade on the left. The city skyline is visible in the distance through a slight haze. A red delivery truck passes by on the road to the right.

segments.scene_description: The character walks between two massive concrete bridge towers under open sky. The bridge railing continues along the left while a billboard for ATMs becomes visible in the distance ahead.

segments.scene_description: The character passes two construction workers standing on the left sidewalk. A brick building with arched windows appears on the left, and a gas station becomes visible ahead as the bridge transitions into the urban area.

segments.scene_description: The character exits the bridge and enters a busy urban intersection. A gas station is prominent on the right beneath a highway overpass. Traffic increases with a white van visible on the road, and commercial billboards line the street.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement as a character traverses a concrete bridge that gradually transitions into a bustling urban intersection. It begins on a bridge walkway beside a stone balustrade, moving past a red delivery truck while the city skyline looms through a slight haze. As the camera proceeds, the environment opens up between two massive concrete towers under a clear sky, bringing a distant ATM billboard into view. The scene then shifts toward the city's edge, passing construction workers and a brick building with arched windows before the bridge merges into the street level. Finally, the camera enters a busy intersection beneath a highway overpass, where the view expands to include a prominent gas station, increasing traffic with a white van, and various commercial billboards.
